channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Fortnite 'Save the World' goes free-to-play
item.content:encoded:
Save the World, Fortnite's original mode that preceded the popular Battle Royale, will become free-to-play next month.

Announced earlier today, the change will come into effect on April 16. Developer Epic Games is currently offering players in-game rewards for pre-registering for the launch. In addition, as a thank you for "the last nine years of holding back the horde," current players will receive an assortment of items.

Before the release of Battle Royale, Save the World's commercial model subsisted on microtransactions. In 2020, Epic ended the early access period for the mode and made it a full release. Rather than making it free-to-play like its Battle Royale counterpart, it remained a premium title within the Fortnite ecosystem.

The news comes merely a day after Epic announced a price increase of V-Bucks, the main currency in Fortnite, to "help pay the bills."

Now, players will get fewer V-Bucks for the same amount of money the currency was previously available for. These price changes go into effect on March 19, when the next Fortnite season begins.

Related:Valve says 5,863 titles earned over $100,000 on Steam in 2025

The Epic Rewards program will give players 20 percent back for V-Bucks purchases, and that credit can be used in Fortnite, other Epic Games titles, or on the Epic Games Store. V-Bucks gift cards that have already been purchased are still valued at the original amount.

In today's announcement, Epic clarified that Save the World founders—those who've paid to play the game—will continue earning V-Bucks through daily quests, mission alerts, storm shield defense missions, and existing challenges.

In addition to V-Bucks, Show-Bucks, the currency for Fall Guys, is also getting a price increase starting April 8. Epic acquired Tonic Games Group, the makers of Fall Guys, back in 2021.

In other related Epic Games news, the company is suing a former contractor for allegedly leaking unreleased Fortnite content across social media. Then, it also announced a Fortnite collab program to bolster store sales, with the likes of Capcom and miHoYo being leveraged by the "marketing power" of the game. If you were wondering about the long-lasting Epic versus Google lawsuit, a Federal Judge cast doubts on the settlement terms back in November of last year.

About the Author

Contributing Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Diego Nicolás Argüello is a freelance journalist and critic from Argentina. Video games helped him to learn English, so now he covers them for places like The New York Times, NPR, Rolling Stone, and more. He also runs Into the Spine, a site dedicated to fostering and supporting new writers, and co-hosted Turnabout Breakdown, a podcast about the Ace Attorney series. He’s most likely playing a rhythm game as you read this.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/fortnite-save-the-world-goes-free-to-play
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Microsoft lifts the lid on Project Helix at GDC Festival of Gaming
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

March 11, 2026

3 Min Read

Microsoft shared more technical details about its next generation console, codenamed Project Helix, at GDC Festival of Gaming. 

Xbox VP of next generation, Jason Ronald, kicked off a day of talks by explaining how the device—which Microsoft already confirmed will play both console and PC titles—will serve developers.

"Project Helix is designed to play your Xbox console and PC games, delivering high performance and providing the ultimate player-first experience. We're partnering closely with AMD to define the next generation of rendering and simulation," said Ronald. 

Project Helix will be powered by a custom AMD-based SOC and is co-designed for the next generation of DirectX. According to Ronald, the console will "bring intelligence directly into the graphics and compute pipeline" to deliver step change functions and gains in efficiency, scale, and visual ambition. 

It will include an "order of magnitude" increase in ray tracing performance and capability, beyond what's currently possible with Xbox Series X | S, while unlocking GPU directed work graph execution to eliminate CPU bottlenecks. 

Related:Sumo Digital partners with Arm to test AI-powered neural graphics tech

Ronald said this means the GPU can generate its own workloads in real time to deliver a "massive uplift" in performance and enable massive real-time simulations that involves complex worlds using runtime generated geometry and large scale interactive worlds. The console has also been designed for the "next generation of neural-assisted rendering," according to Ronald. 

"We've reached some of the limitations of what's possible with traditional rendering techniques, and if we want to continue advancing the state of the art we have to invent brand new technology," he added. 

Key to achieving that aim will be the integration of the next version of AMD FSR into the Project Helix development kit. 

"This is really designed for that next generation of neural rendering techniques. Whether that's neural materials. Whether that's generated images. Or even if you think about things like the latest ML (machine learning) based upscaling techniques or super resolution techniques," said Ronald. 

"You think about brand new ML based multi-frame generation. There's even new capabilities, such as a brand new ray regeneration technique that's really designed to deliver high performance ray tracing for both real-time ray tracing and path tracing." 

Another area Microsoft is focused on with Project Helix is what the company calls "deep texture compression," which Ronald claims will allow developers to continue pushing boundaries while levering the hardware in more efficient ways. Notably, he said this could be a key feature as storage and memory prices continue to swell. 

Related:Outersloth shares contract terms and game funding lessons for sickos

"There's a ton of work that we can do on the hardware side. There's a ton of work that we can do on the software side, but it's also about how you take advantage of this as developers. And so when we talk about deep texture compression, that includes the latest neural texture compression techniques, but we're also leaning in very heavily into Z standard as well," said Ronald. 

"So, that is a capability that allows you to use the latest version of direct storage and be able to stream assets directly off of the storage drive and be much more sensitive in how you're actually using memory, because you can actually stream it directly off of the SSD itself." 

So, when will developers get their hands on the hardware? Ronald said Microsoft will begin sending alpha versions of Project Helix to developers starting in 2027. Admittedly, that's a big window, but at least it's there in black and white. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/production/microsoft-lifts-the-lid-on-project-helix-at-gdc-festival-of-gaming
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
